stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 investment portfolio options. The probability of favorable market conditions is uncertain, estimated between 30% and 45%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: bonds: $54K in good market conditions, $12K in bad market conditions. index fund: $298K in good market conditions, $129K in bad market conditions. tech stocks: $108K in good market conditions, $14K in bad market conditions. real estate: $159K in good market conditions, $28K in bad market conditions. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.38: best option is index fund with EV=$193.2K. index fund is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [179.7, 205.1].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
index fund is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
Not performing minimax regret analysis to find the most robust choice
